title: 🌵 Amazon Top Ten Indoor Plants
excerpt: Looking to transform your living space Looking to transform your living space with greenery but not…
body:
Looking to transform your living space

Looking to transform your living space with greenery but not sure where to start? This guide covers Amazon’s top ten indoor plants perfect for beginners, busy professionals, and anyone wanting to bring nature indoors without the stress.

We’ll break down low-maintenance champions like the Snake Plant and ZZ Plant that thrive on neglect, plus show you stunning statement pieces like the Monstera Deliciosa and Fiddle Leaf Fig that’ll make your home Instagram-ready. You’ll also discover practical picks like pet-safe Spider Plants and the healing powers of Aloe Vera, so you can choose plants that fit your lifestyle and space perfectly.

Find out more from the links below

https://bloom-curated.lovable.app/

Snake Plant – The Ultimate Low Maintenance Choice

Survives in low light and infrequent watering

Snake plants thrive in situations that would kill most other houseplants. These hardy beauties can handle the darkest corners of your home where other plants would struggle and eventually die. Whether you place them in a dimly lit bathroom, a windowless office cubicle, or that shadowy spot behind your couch, they’ll keep growing steadily without complaint.

The watering schedule for snake plants is refreshingly simple – water them every 2-3 weeks during the growing season and even less frequently in winter. Their thick, succulent-like leaves store water efficiently, making them drought-tolerant champions. You can actually harm them more by overwatering than by forgetting to water them for a month. This makes them perfect for busy professionals, frequent travelers, or anyone who’s killed plants with kindness before.

Purifies air while you sleep

Snake plants perform a unique trick that sets them apart from most houseplants – they release oxygen at night instead of during the day. While most plants switch to consuming oxygen after dark, snake plants continue their air-purifying work around the clock. NASA’s Clean Air Study specifically highlighted snake plants for removing formaldehyde, xylene, benzene, and trichloroethylene from indoor air.

Placing a snake plant in your bedroom creates a natural air purification system that works while you rest. The plant absorbs carbon dioxide and releases fresh oxygen throughout the night, potentially improving your sleep quality. Many people report sleeping better with snake plants in their bedrooms, though the oxygen boost is subtle rather than dramatic.

Grows upright without taking floor space

Snake plants grow in tight, vertical clusters that maximize their visual impact while minimizing their footprint. Their sword-like leaves shoot straight up, creating dramatic vertical lines that draw the eye upward and make rooms feel taller. A mature snake plant can reach 3-4 feet in height while occupying less than a square foot of floor space.

This space-efficient growth pattern makes them ideal for small apartments, narrow hallways, or any room where floor space is precious. You can’t tuck them into corners, place them between furniture pieces, or use them as natural room dividers. Their architectural form complements both modern minimalist and traditional decor styles, adding sculptural interest without overwhelming the space.

Pothos – Fast Growing Trailing Beauty

Thrives in Any Lighting Condition

Pothos plants are incredibly adaptable when it comes to light requirements, making them perfect for virtually any space in your home. Whether you have a bright, sunny window or a dimly lit corner, your pothos will adjust and continue growing. In bright, indirect light, you’ll notice faster growth and more vibrant variegation in varieties like Golden Pothos or Marble Queen. Low light conditions slow growth, but won’t harm the plant – it simply goes into conservation mode.

The beauty of pothos lies in its tolerance for less-than-perfect conditions. Office cubicles with fluorescent lighting, bathrooms with small windows, or even bedrooms with minimal natural light can all support a thriving pothos. This flexibility makes them ideal starter plants for beginners who might not have the perfect growing conditions figured out yet.

Propagates Easily for Endless Plants

Creating new pothos plants is surprisingly simple and rewarding. Cut a 4-6 inch stem section that includes at least one node (the small bump where leaves emerge), and you’re ready to propagate. Place the cutting in water, and within a week or two, you’ll see roots developing. Once roots reach about an inch long, plant them in soil.

This easy propagation method means you can expand your plant collection without spending extra money. Many plant enthusiasts start with one pothos and end up with dozens through propagation. You can also share cuttings with friends and family, making pothos an excellent gift that keeps giving. The success rate for pothos propagation is remarkably high, even for complete beginners.

Removes Harmful Toxins from Indoor Air

NASA’s Clean Air Study identified pothos as one of the top air-purifying plants for indoor spaces. These plants naturally filter out common household toxins, including formaldehyde, xylene, and toluene – chemicals often found in furniture, carpets, and cleaning products. While you’d need multiple plants to significantly impact air quality in larger spaces, every little bit helps create a healthier indoor environment.

The air-purifying process happens through the plant’s natural transpiration and absorption processes. Pothos leaves and roots work together to break down harmful compounds and release clean oxygen back into your home. This natural air filtration system works 24/7, providing continuous benefits for your indoor air quality.

Perfect for Hanging Baskets or Shelves

Pothos plants are natural climbers in their native habitat, but indoors they create stunning trailing displays. Their vines can grow several feet long, creating dramatic cascading effects from hanging baskets or high shelves. The heart-shaped leaves grow along the entire length of the vine, maintaining visual interest from top to bottom.

Hanging displays work particularly well because they allow the vines to trail naturally while keeping the plant out of reach of pets and small children. High shelf placement also takes advantage of rising warm air, which pothos appreciate. You can train the vines around doorframes, along mantels, or even up walls using small hooks or plant clips for creative living wall effects.

Peace Lily – Elegant Flowering Air Purifier

Blooms Beautiful White Flowers Indoors

Peace lilies stand out as one of the few houseplants that consistently produce stunning white blooms indoors. These elegant spathes (technically modified leaves that look like petals) emerge throughout the year when the plant receives proper care. The white flowers create a striking contrast against the plant’s glossy dark green foliage, adding a touch of sophistication to any room.

The blooming cycle typically lasts 2-3 months, with each flower gradually transitioning from pure white to green before fading. With adequate bright, indirect light and consistent moisture, mature peace lilies can produce multiple blooms simultaneously. Young plants may take 1-2 years to reach blooming maturity, but the wait is absolutely worth it when those first pristine white flowers appear.

Indicates Watering Needs Through Drooping

Peace lilies have developed an incredibly helpful communication system with their owners – dramatic drooping. When the soil becomes too dry, the leaves will visibly wilt and hang down, creating an unmistakable signal that it’s time to water. This natural indicator takes the guesswork out of plant care for beginners.

Within hours of watering, the leaves perk back up to their normal upright position, almost like magic. This drooping behavior occurs because the plant redirects water from its leaves to conserve moisture in its roots and stems. While occasional drooping won’t harm the plant, consistently allowing it to wilt can stress the peace lily and reduce flowering.

The key is catching the droop early – when leaves just begin to look less perky rather than completely collapsed. Water thoroughly until excess drains from the bottom, then allow the soil to dry slightly between waterings.

Filters Out Dangerous Household Chemicals

NASA’s Clean Air Study identified peace lilies as exceptional air purifiers, capable of removing multiple toxic compounds from indoor environments. These plants excel at filtering out benzene, formaldehyde, trichloroethylene, xylene, and ammonia – common pollutants found in household cleaners, paints, furniture, and building materials.

The plant’s large, broad leaves act like natural air filters, absorbing these chemicals through their pores and breaking them down in their root systems. A single mature peace lily can clean approximately 100 square feet of living space, making it an excellent choice for bedrooms, living rooms, or home offices.

Beyond chemical filtration, peace lilies also increase humidity levels through transpiration, helping counteract the dry air common in heated and air-conditioned homes. This dual benefit of toxin removal and humidity regulation creates a healthier indoor environment while adding natural beauty to your space.

Rubber Plant – Bold Statement Piece

Large glossy leaves create a dramatic focal point

The Rubber Plant’s most striking feature has to be those incredible, oversized leaves that look like nature’s own leather masterpiece. Each leaf can grow up to 12 inches long and 6 inches wide, with a deep emerald green color and a glossy finish that catches light beautifully throughout the day. When you place a Rubber Plant in your living room or office, it instantly becomes the star of the show – no other décor piece needed.

The leaves emerge from sturdy branches in an alternating pattern, creating this amazing architectural structure that designers absolutely love. Young leaves start with a reddish tint before maturing into that signature dark green, giving you this cool color progression as your plant grows. The waxy surface of each leaf reflects light in different directions, making your space feel brighter and more dynamic.

What makes these plants such perfect statement pieces is their ability to fill vertical space without taking up much floor area. A single Rubber Plant can easily reach 6-10 feet indoors, creating this impressive column of green that draws the eye upward and makes rooms feel larger.

Tolerates neglect and low light conditions

Busy plant parents rejoice – Rubber Plants are basically the golden retrievers of the houseplant world. They’re incredibly forgiving if you forget to water them for a week or two. These plants actually prefer their soil to dry out between waterings, so that “oops, I forgot to water my plants” moment won’t kill your Rubber Plant.

Low light situations that would make other plants sulk and drop leaves? Not a problem for Rubber Plants. They can handle everything from bright indirect light to those dimmer corners of your home where other plants fear to grow. While they’ll grow faster in brighter conditions, they’ll still thrive in that north-facing window or office cubicle with fluorescent lighting.

Temperature fluctuations don’t faze them much either. They’re comfortable in typical indoor temperatures between 60-75°F and can handle occasional drafts or temperature swings that might stress other tropical plants. This makes them perfect for homes with inconsistent heating or air conditioning.

Removes formaldehyde from indoor air

NASA’s Clean Air Study identified Rubber Plants as one of the top performers for removing formaldehyde from indoor air. Formaldehyde lurks in many household items – from furniture and carpeting to cleaning products and pressed wood. Your Rubber Plant works around the clock, quietly filtering these chemicals through its large leaf surface area.

The bigger the plant, the more air purification power you get. Those massive leaves aren’t just for show – they’re working overtime to process the air in your home. A mature Rubber Plant can clean the air in a room up to 100 square feet, making it perfect for bedrooms, living rooms, or home offices.

Easy pruning maintains the desired size

Don’t want your Rubber Plant taking over your living room? No worries. These plants respond beautifully to pruning, allowing you to keep them at whatever size works for your space. The best time to prune is during spring or early summer when the plant is actively growing.

When you cut a branch, new growth will emerge just below the cut, often creating two or three new branches from one cut point. This means you can encourage bushier growth or maintain a specific height with strategic pruning. Clean, sharp scissors or pruning shears work best, and always wipe the milky sap that emerges from cut stems.

Spider Plant – Pet Safe Hanging Favorite

Safe around cats and dogs

Spider plants top the list when you’re looking for greenery that won’t harm your furry friends. These cheerful plants contain zero toxic compounds, making them completely safe if your curious cat decides to nibble on the leaves or your puppy gets a little too playful. Pet parents can breathe easy knowing their four-legged family members can explore freely without any health risks.

Many plant enthusiasts with pets struggle to find beautiful options that don’t require constant vigilance. Spider plants solve this problem perfectly, allowing you to enjoy lush foliage while your pets roam safely throughout your home. The plant’s non-toxic nature extends to all parts – leaves, stems, and even the baby plantlets are harmless.

Produces baby plants for sharing

One of the most delightful features of spider plants is their generous nature. Mature plants develop long, arching stems called stolons that produce miniature versions of themselves – adorable baby spider plants complete with their own root systems. These little offshoots, often called “spiderettes” or “plantlets,” dangle from the mother plant like tiny green ornaments.

You can propagate these babies effortlessly by placing small pots of water or soil beneath them while they’re still attached to the parent plant. Within weeks, they develop strong roots and can be separated to create new plants. This natural multiplication makes spider plants perfect for sharing with friends, expanding your own collection, or giving as thoughtful housewarming gifts.

Adapts to various light conditions

Spider plants demonstrate remarkable flexibility when it comes to lighting requirements. They thrive in bright, indirect sunlight but adapt surprisingly well to lower light situations, making them suitable for offices, bathrooms, or rooms with limited natural light. This adaptability means you won’t need to rearrange your entire living space to accommodate their needs.

Direct sunlight can actually harm spider plants by scorching their leaves, so they prefer the gentle, filtered light found a few feet away from windows. They’ll even tolerate fluorescent office lighting, though their growth may slow slightly in these conditions. This versatility makes them excellent starter plants for beginners who are still learning about plant placement.

Improves indoor humidity levels

Spider plants act as natural humidifiers through their transpiration process, releasing moisture into the air through their leaves. This quality proves especially valuable during dry winter months when indoor heating systems strip moisture from the air, leaving skin dry and respiratory systems irritated.

A single spider plant can release several ounces of water into the surrounding air daily, contributing to a more comfortable indoor environment. This natural humidity boost benefits both human health and other houseplants in the vicinity, creating a more balanced ecosystem within your home.

ZZ Plant – Virtually Indestructible Option

Survives weeks without water

The ZZ plant earned its reputation as a drought champion for good reason. This resilient beauty can go 2-4 weeks without a single drop of water, making it perfect for busy professionals, frequent travelers, or anyone who occasionally forgets their watering schedule. The secret lies in its thick, fleshy rhizomes that store water underground, similar to how a cactus operates.

What makes this plant truly remarkable is its ability to actually prefer neglect over attention. Overwatering kills more ZZ plants than underwatering ever will. The plant’s waxy, succulent-like leaves also help retain moisture, creating a self-sustaining system that laughs in the face of forgetful plant parents.

Thrives in fluorescent office lighting

Most plants wilt under harsh fluorescent lights, but the ZZ plant sees them as a five-star resort. Originally from Eastern Africa, this plant evolved under the canopy of larger trees, adapting to filtered, indirect light conditions that mimic typical office environments perfectly.

Your ZZ plant will happily photosynthesize under those buzzing fluorescent tubes that make everyone else look pale and tired. It doesn’t need a sunny windowsill or expensive grow lights – just the ambient lighting of your workspace will keep it thriving. This makes it an ideal desk companion that won’t judge your windowless cubicle.

Glossy leaves stay dust-free naturally

The ZZ plant’s leaves have a natural waxy coating that repels dust like a built-in cleaning system. While other houseplants collect dust and require regular wiping, ZZ plant leaves maintain their mirror-like shine with minimal intervention. This waxy surface not only keeps the plant looking pristine but also helps protect against pests and diseases.

When dust does occasionally settle, it wipes away effortlessly with a damp cloth, revealing those gorgeous, glossy green leaves that reflect light beautifully around your space.

Monstera Deliciosa – Instagram Worthy Split Leaves

Develops stunning fenestrated leaves with age

Young Monstera Deliciosa plants start life with simple, heart-shaped leaves that look surprisingly ordinary. But patience pays off big time with this tropical beauty. As your plant matures, typically around 2-3 years old, something magical happens – those iconic splits and holes begin appearing. The scientific term “fenestrations” sounds fancy, but these natural perforations create the Swiss cheese appearance that makes Monstera so instantly recognizable.

The fenestration process happens gradually, with each new leaf becoming more dramatically split than the last. Younger plants might develop just a few small holes, while mature specimens can produce leaves with deep, artistic cuts that extend almost to the stem. These aren’t random – the plant creates these openings as an adaptation to strong winds in its native rainforest habitat.

Climbs moss poles for impressive height

Monstera Deliciosa is naturally a climbing vine that can reach towering heights when given proper support. Installing a moss pole transforms your plant from a sprawling houseplant into a stunning vertical statement piece. The aerial roots that develop along the stem will actually grab onto the moss, creating a secure attachment that mimics how the plant grows in nature.

Without support, your Monstera will spread horizontally across surfaces, which limits leaf size and fenestration development. But give it a moss pole to climb, and you’ll see dramatic improvements in both leaf size and split patterns. Plants that climb can develop leaves over two feet wide, compared to much smaller foliage on unsupported specimens.

Adds tropical ambiance to any room

Nothing says “tropical paradise” quite like a mature Monstera with its glossy, sculptural leaves. This plant instantly transforms any space into a lush, jungle-inspired retreat. The large, dramatic foliage creates natural focal points that work beautifully in modern, bohemian, or traditional decor styles.

Interior designers love Monstera for its ability to soften harsh lines and add organic texture to minimalist spaces. The deep green color provides a calming backdrop, while the unique leaf shapes create visual interest without overwhelming smaller rooms. Place one in a corner, and it becomes living art that changes and grows over time.

Tolerates medium to bright indirect light

Monstera Deliciosa adapts well to various lighting conditions, making it more forgiving than many tropical plants. Bright, indirect light produces the best growth and fenestration, but these plants can handle medium light situations too. East or north-facing windows provide ideal conditions, while south or west windows should be filtered with sheer curtains.

Watch your plant’s leaves for lighting cues. New growth that’s solid green without splits usually means the plant needs more light. Yellowing leaves or brown patches indicate too much direct sun. The sweet spot produces those coveted fenestrated leaves with rich, deep green coloring that makes your Monstera truly Instagram-worthy.

Fiddle Leaf Fig – Designer Favorite Tree

Creates stunning floor-to-ceiling presence

The Fiddle Leaf Fig transforms any room into an upscale botanical paradise with its impressive vertical growth. These architectural plants can reach heights of 6-10 feet indoors, creating a dramatic living sculpture that commands attention from every corner of your space. Their tall, single-trunk structure makes them perfect for filling empty corners, framing windows, or anchoring a seating area with natural elegance.

What sets these plants apart is their ability to create multiple levels of visual interest. The thick, sturdy trunk provides a strong foundation while the canopy of large leaves creates a lush umbrella effect above. This vertical drama works especially well in rooms with high ceilings, where the plant can truly stretch and showcase its natural tree-like form.

Many interior designers use Fiddle Leaf Figs as living room focal points because they provide the same impact as a piece of art while adding fresh oxygen to your space. The plant’s upright growth habit means it won’t sprawl across surfaces or take up valuable floor real estate, making it ideal for modern homes where space efficiency matters.

Large violin-shaped leaves make bold statement

The signature foliage of the Fiddle Leaf Fig resembles violin bodies, with their wide, rounded tops tapering to narrower bases. Each leaf can grow 12-18 inches long and 8-10 inches wide, creating substantial visual weight that anchors any room’s design scheme. The deep forest green color provides a rich backdrop that complements both neutral and bold color palettes.

These glossy leaves catch and reflect light beautifully, changing appearance throughout the day as natural light shifts across their surfaces. The prominent veining creates additional texture and pattern, adding sophisticated detail that holds up under close inspection. Unlike plants with small, busy foliage, the Fiddle Leaf Fig’s large leaves create clean lines and uncluttered visual impact.

The leaves grow in an alternating pattern along the branches, creating natural asymmetry that feels organic rather than formal. New growth emerges as bright green shoots that gradually darken and expand, providing ongoing visual interest as your plant develops. This dynamic growth pattern means your Fiddle Leaf Fig evolves its appearance over time, never becoming static or boring in your decor.

Thrives with consistent watering schedule

Success with Fiddle Leaf Figs comes down to establishing a reliable watering routine that mimics their native habitat. These plants prefer deep, thorough watering followed by a drying period, rather than frequent light waterings that can lead to root rot. Check the top inch of soil weekly – when it feels dry to the touch, it’s time for a generous drink.

Water slowly and deeply until you see drainage from the bottom holes, ensuring the entire root system receives moisture. Empty the drainage tray after 30 minutes to prevent the plant from sitting in standing water. During growing season (spring and summer), you’ll typically water every 7-10 days, while winter watering may stretch to every 2-3 weeks.

Consistency matters more than perfect timing. Fiddle Leaf Figs respond poorly to erratic watering schedules that swing between drought and flood conditions. Keep a simple calendar reminder or use a plant care app to track your watering dates. Room temperature, filtered water works best, as cold water can shock the roots and chlorinated tap water may cause leaf spotting over time.

Aloe Vera – Healing Plant with Multiple Benefits

Provides natural remedy for burns and cuts

Aloe vera contains a clear gel packed with healing compounds that make it nature’s first aid kit. This succulent has been used for thousands of years to treat everything from minor burns and cuts to sunburn and dry skin. The gel inside the thick, fleshy leaves contains over 75 active compounds, including vitamins, minerals, amino acids, and anti-inflammatory agents that speed up healing.

When you have a kitchen burn or small cut, simply break off a leaf and squeeze out the cooling gel directly onto the affected area. The plant’s natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain, prevent infection, and promote faster healing. Many people keep an aloe plant in their kitchen specifically for this purpose – it’s like having a natural pharmacy right on your windowsill.

Requires minimal watering and care

Aloe vera thrives on neglect, making it perfect for busy lifestyles or forgetful waterers. These desert natives store water in their thick leaves, so they can go weeks without a drink. Water deeply but infrequently – about once every 2-3 weeks during growing season and even less in winter.

The key is well-draining soil and avoiding overwatering, which can cause root rot. Plant your aloe in a cactus mix or regular potting soil mixed with perlite. These plants prefer bright, indirect sunlight and temperatures between 60-75°F. Yellow or brown leaves usually indicate too much water, while shriveled leaves mean it needs a drink.

Produces new shoots for plant multiplication

Aloe plants are generous gift-givers, regularly producing baby plants called “pups” or offsets around their base. These miniature clones share the same root system as the mother plant and can be easily separated to create new plants. This natural propagation method means you’ll never run out of aloe – and you’ll have plenty to share with friends.

To separate pups, gently remove the parent plant from its pot and look for babies with their own root systems. Use a clean knife to cut the connecting root, let the cut dry for a day, then pot each pup in its own container. Within a few weeks, you’ll have independent aloe plants ready to start their own healing journey.

Adds sculptural element to sunny windowsills

Beyond its practical benefits, aloe vera brings striking architectural beauty to indoor spaces. The plant’s rosette formation creates dramatic shadows and interesting geometric patterns that change throughout the day as light moves across the thick, pointed leaves. The blue-green coloration with subtle variations adds depth and visual interest to any room.

Mature aloe plants develop an impressive presence, with some varieties reaching 2-3 feet tall. Their sculptural quality works especially well in modern, minimalist decor where clean lines and natural textures are valued. Place them in bright windows where their unique form can be showcased while they soak up the sunlight they crave.

Philodendron Heartleaf – Heart Shaped Trailing Wonder

Heart-shaped leaves bring romantic charm

The Philodendron Heartleaf gets its name from those adorable heart-shaped leaves that cascade gracefully from hanging baskets or climb up moss poles. Each glossy green leaf measures about 2-4 inches and creates an instant romantic atmosphere in any room. The leaves start as bronze-colored baby leaves before maturing into that classic deep emerald green that makes this plant so recognizable.

What makes this plant extra special is how the leaves seem to dance as they trail down from their container. The vines can grow several feet long, creating living curtains that soften harsh corners and add organic curves to modern spaces. You’ll often catch yourself just staring at those perfect heart shapes – they’re nature’s way of spreading love throughout your home.

The foliage stays vibrant year-round, maintaining that rich color even in lower light conditions. Unlike some plants that get leggy or lose their appeal over time, the Heartleaf Philodendron just keeps getting more beautiful as it grows, with each new leaf adding to its romantic charm.

Grows quickly in water or soil

This plant is incredibly versatile when it comes to growing mediums. You can propagate cuttings in a simple glass of water and watch roots develop within weeks. Many plant lovers keep their Philodendron Heartleaf permanently in water, changing it every week or two, and the plant thrives just fine this way.

For soil growing, use a well-draining potting mix that retains some moisture but doesn’t get soggy. The plant adapts quickly to new soil conditions and shows rapid growth during spring and summer months. You’ll notice new leaves unfurling every few weeks when conditions are right.

The growth rate is impressive – vines can extend 4-6 inches per month during peak growing season. This makes it perfect for filling empty spaces quickly or creating that lush jungle vibe people love on social media.

Perfect for beginners with forgiving nature

New plant parents absolutely love this species because it communicates its needs clearly without being dramatic. When it needs water, the leaves will droop slightly – but bounce back quickly after a drink. If you forget to water for a week or two, it won’t throw a tantrum like some fussier plants.

The Heartleaf tolerates a wide range of light conditions, from bright indirect light to lower light corners where other plants might struggle. It’s also forgiving about humidity levels, though it does appreciate occasional misting.

Mistakes rarely kill this plant. Overwatering might cause some yellow leaves, but it recovers fast with adjusted care. Even if you accidentally break off a vine, you can root it in water and have a new plant to share with friends.

Amazon’s top ten indoor plants offer something perfect for every home and skill level. From the nearly indestructible ZZ plant that thrives on neglect to the show-stopping Monstera with its gorgeous split leaves, these plants prove that bringing nature indoors doesn’t have to be complicated. Whether you’re drawn to the air-purifying Peace Lily, the pet-safe Spider Plant, or the healing properties of Aloe Vera, each option brings its own special charm to your living space.

Ready to transform your home into a green oasis? Start with one or two plants that match your lifestyle and experience level. The Snake Plant or Pothos make excellent beginner choices, while the Fiddle Leaf Fig can be your next challenge once you’ve gained confidence. Your indoor jungle awaits – and with these tried-and-true favorites, you’re guaranteed to find plants that not only survive but thrive in your care.
